what_it_does: This file is a JSON ledger of typographic and content-budget findings extracted from pitch decks and landing-page designs. Each entry names a type role or emergent text pattern, describes its visual recipe, cites slide evidence, states a generator rule, and tags the source deck and compression depth.
observations: Entries are categorized as type-hierarchy, emergent-narrative-title-chain, or emergent-content-budget.
Every finding pairs a descriptive recipe with an actionable generator_rule and evidence citations.
Sources include capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
Depths range from 1-page overviews through 15-page, 18-page, 30-page, and 49-page decks.
Recurring type roles: H1 slide title (near-black, bold, centred/left), gold section heads, gold oversized stat numbers, body bullets with gold arrow markers, captions, labels, and CTA buttons.

Several entries are flagged emergent: title-chain narrative, strict one-line headings, intentional bullet-length calibration, single-sans typeface system, status dots, italic promise lines, bold-italic product terms.
Generator rules specify concrete content budgets in characters, words, and lines.
Evidence uses inconsistent slide reference styles (p-02, p02, p18, p-36).
standouts: Titles form a single continuous grammatical sentence across the deck via connective openings.
Strict one-line rule for headings, labels, buttons, and most bullets.
Typeface system confirmed as a single sans-serif family across all text roles.
Product status dot + label is the only permitted use of green.
Italic promise lines and bold-italic product terms create distinct voice registers.
Large data figures are consistently gold and paired with smaller dark captions.
conventions: Uses a fixed schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Recipe describes font, weight, size, color, alignment, and spacing in approximate terms.
Generator_rule translates observations into prescriptive content and style constraints.
Gold is reserved for section labels, stats, names, and accents; near-black for primary claims and body.
Emergent flag distinguishes observed patterns from explicitly designed rules.
Source and depth metadata keep findings traceable to their origin deck.
concerns: Size and color values are approximate and eye-estimated, not measured from design tokens.
Multiple overlapping entries for the same role across sources use slightly different numeric ranges, which could conflict if fused naively.
No explicit resolution strategy for cross-source discrepancies.
Slide reference notation is inconsistent within evidence strings.
The file contains no symbols list, so downstream symbol indexing must treat it as data-only.
notes: A dense, source-annotated typographic pattern book that treats text not just as style but as measured content budgets tied to deck compression.
questions: Are these findings manually authored, auto-extracted, or a hybrid?
How should conflicting numeric ranges for the same type role across sources be reconciled?
Is the intended consumer a generator, a reviewer, or both?
Do _depth values map to a canonical compression scale, or are they source-specific labels?
Should approximate color/size values be normalized to design tokens later?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as a typographic and content-budget pattern ledger; its actual content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Color roles separate claims, proof, and accents.
Compression depth changes content budget but preserves hierarchy.
Emergent patterns are as important as explicit style rules.
intention_signals: To encode a reusable, source-aware design grammar so that generated decks and pages reproduce consistent hierarchy, color usage, and prose length limits across different compression levels.
novelty: The combination of visual type hierarchy with hard content-budget constraints and emergent narrative structures, such as the continuous-sentence title chain and calibrated bullet lengths.
contribution: Provides granular, source-annotated typography and content-budget findings that a fused system can use to enforce hierarchy, color roles, line-length limits, and emergent narrative constraints without favoring any single source.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of typographic hierarchy and content-budget findings extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page designs, each entry documenting visual recipe, evidence, generator rule, source, and compression depth.
intention_for_embedding: To capture reusable type-system rules and prose-length constraints for generating or reviewing consistent presentation and landing-page typography.
